design_philosophy.core_loop: Survive waves of alien/insect enemies while managing weapon selection and screen space; continuous forward momentum with tactical dodging
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Bullet density vs. player maneuverability; resource scarcity in weapon ammo/power-ups
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Lone soldier defending Earth against overwhelming alien invasion; progression from vulnerable to empowered through weapon acquisition
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strong commitment to shmup arcade traditions: no difficulty scaling, punishing enemy patterns, weapon management as core strategic layer rather than cosmetic progression

difficulty.lives_system: Limited lives with continue system; death resets to checkpoint or level start
difficulty.checkpointing: Stage-based checkpointing; continues restart at stage beginning or mid-stage checkpoint
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Minimal difficulty settings; player agency primarily through weapon selection between stages and tactical dodging within stages

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon acquisition and cycling system; different weapons with distinct firing patterns and ammo costs; no permanent stat upgrades
progression.unlock_mechanism: Weapon unlocks through stage progression and pickup collection during play
replayability_hooks: Multiple weapon paths to explore across stages
Different enemy patterns requiring pattern recognition on replays

world_building_techniques: Enemy type design establishes alien invasion scenario (insect/creature enemies)
Stage locations (military bases, cities, landscapes) imply escalating invasion scope
Title and premise establish Earth Defense Force framing without narrative exposition

boss_design.philosophy: Pattern-based confrontations serving as stage climaxes; designed to test mastery of weapon selection and dodge mechanics learned in stage

faction_indicators: Unified Earth Defense Force (implied player alignment)
environmental_storytelling: Military installation stages indicate organized defense efforts
Urban environments show civilian involvement in defense
Escalating alien creature complexity suggests threat evolution
